title: Custom exceptions should inherit from Exception or one of its subclasses
description: Custom exceptions should inherit from the built-in Exception class or one of its subclasses.
message: Exception class '{{ full_class_name }}' does not inherit from Exception or one of its subclasses.
recommendation: Inherit custom exceptions from Exception or one of its subclasses.

noncompliant.description: Inheriting from object instead of Exception.

compliant.code:
from Exception import Exception
class CustomException(Exception): pass
compliant.description: Inheriting from the built-in Exception class.

compliant.code:
from StandardError import StandardError
class CustomException(StandardError): pass
compliant.description: Inheriting from a subclass of Exception.

why_is_this_an_issue: Using custom exceptions that do not inherit from Exception or one of its subclasses can make it difficult to handle exceptions consistently and can lead to errors when trying to use built-in exception handling features.
how_to_fix: Inherit custom exceptions from the built-in Exception class or one of its subclasses.
